description: Description of this distribution, e.g. what format, access constraints, etc.
MIME type with extension; should indicate the serialization scheme in sufficient detail that machine can know how to parse.
A string value calculated from the content of the resource representation, used to test if content has been modified. The checksum is a property of a particular distribution/DataDownload.
An identifier for a standard or specification that the distribution conforms to. Recommended to enable machine-actionable data access.
Party who maintains this particular distribution option for the dataset. Use this property if there are multiple distributions from different providers
string label for person that is meaningful for human users
identifier for person, recommend ORCID
In this context for the schema:PropertyValue, this field is an identifier for the identifier schema, e.g. DOI, ARK.  Get values from https://registry.identifiers.org/registry/ for interoperability
the identifier string. E.g. 10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
web-resolveable string for the identifier; host name part is location of a resolver that will return some representation for the given identifier value. E.g. https://doi.org/10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
other labels by which the person might be known
if affiliation is present, value must be a schema:Organization.
text label for the term that is useful to human user

other classifier for the file. ada componentTypes are specified in the specific file type schema attached by $refs
Links between files in the product. Use schema:name for path to target in product, or use #id JSON-LD links. Used to link metadata files in bundle to the data or supplementary files they document.
One of the supplemental document types if applicable
Type of illumination used to create the image. Examples include Visible light, Cross-polarized visible light, ultraviolet light, Electron beam, X-ray.
Specifies the nature of the sample's response to the illumination that was detected and measured.
Spectrometers used in analysis
link to appropriate basemap image map
Whether coordinates are stage-defined or pixel-defined. If pixel-defined, are coordinates from stage, upperleftPixel, or centerPixel.
The location of the origin pixel of an image. Range: upperLeft, upperRight, lowerLeft, lowerRight, center
The column names are redundant, they are lists in the hasPhysicalMapping array. Include here for convenience.
Schema constraint is that one of {'isDelimited','isFixedWidth'} must be present with a True value; the other one may be present or omitted, but if present must have a false value.
An atomic property that sets the comment prefix flag to the single provided value, which MUST be a string. The default is '#'.
Sets the delimiter flag to the single provided value, which MUST be a string. The default is ','.
If true, sets the header row count flag to 1, and if false to 0, unless headerRowCount is provided, in which case the value provided for the header property is ignored. The default is true.
A numeric atomic property that sets the header row count flag to the single provided value, which MUST be a non-negative integer.
Same as DDI-CDI quoteCharacter. The string that is used around escaped cells.
The number of columns to skip at the beginning of each row.
The number of rows to skip at the beginning of the file, before a header row or tabular data.
The character used to escape special characters in the data. From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.escapeCharacter.
Whether column header names are case-sensitive. From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.headerIsCaseSensitive.
Whether consecutive delimiters should be treated as a single delimiter. From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.treatConsecutiveDelimitersAsOne.
Indicates the direction of the table layout (left-to-right or right-to-left). From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.tableDirection.

Indicates whether the text within cells should be displayed as left-to-right, right-to-left, according to content, or inherited from the table direction. From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.textDirection.
Indicates whether to trim whitespace around cells. 'true' corresponds to DDI-CDI 'both' value, 'false' corresponds to DDI-CDI 'neither' value.
Links variables to their physical representation in this dataset.
Non-negative integer that orders the fields in the data structure (column number).
A format for number expressed as a string, or date format like YYYY/MM or MM-DD-YY.
The column width if the tabular text is fixed width.
The value of this property becomes the null annotation for the described column.
A default string indicating the value to substitute for an empty string.
Reference to a variable defined in schema:variableMeasured.
List of the component types in the collection
Number of files in the collection, including metadata files
Full path to file in container object
Pattern for collection members with differentiating suffix at '*'
The component type for the file(s)
MIME type with extension
String that can be used by software to locate values of the variable in this physical dataset.
same as ada/samis '_originalName'
Free text explanation of file format, or a link to a publicly accessible specification for the format.
